name: Ops Dashboard — AI Operating System
body: Running a booth business means flying blind between five browser tabs, wondering what actually needs you today. The important signal drowns in the noise.

The Ops Dashboard is a *custom-built overview of your business performance*, designed to streamline operations and keep you focused where the business needs you most: your __Daily Focus__, CRM insights, inbox alerts with interactive buttons, financials and forecasts, and an Overall Business Health view that confirms every system is healthy and humming.
Every build is custom to your business. Starts at __$6,500__ for a fully connected dashboard system.
Limited to *one build per quarter*, 90-day turnaround on average. Serious inquiries only.

name: MIGHTY-CRM
body: Generic CRMs make booth operators fight their assumptions: no event dates, no venue records, pipelines built for SaaS sales instead of Saturday bookings.
MIGHTY-CRM is *purpose-built for photo booths*: leads, quotes, invoicing rails, and a pipeline board that matches how bookings actually flow. Cloud Postgres underneath, a real quote builder on top.

name: Analytics Report — Snappic
description: Branded post-event analytics report for Snappic events.
body: The event ends, the gallery link goes out, and the client's boss asks "was it worth it?" with nothing to point at. That silence kills rebookings.

This turns Snappic event data into a deliverable that answers the question: __branded cover, executive-level briefing__, engagement stats, and peak-time analysis. Computer vision selects the *highlight photos of the event*, a QR code drops the reader into the gallery, and the branded final page closes with a rebooking CTA.
It performs better than what either booth software company offers out of the box. Guaranteed to blow your corporate clients away and set you apart from your competition.

name: Remote Event Monitoring — Snappic
body: A drop-off booth that dies at hour one earns you a furious email at hour five. You can't stand next to every activation, so every unattended event is a slow-motion gamble.

Monitoring agents watch the live Snappic feed and alert you the moment it matters: *idle-booth alerts* when sessions stall, *hype alerts* when the floor heats up, and __system overwatch__ for the quiet killers like an iPad not charging or dropping connection.
You act while the event is still saveable, instead of reading about it in a review.

name: Remote Event Monitoring — Booth.Events
body: Sending staff out alone or dropping a booth means hours of wondering whether anything is actually happening. Silence is not reassurance.

Monitoring agents watch the live Booth.Events feed and send custom alerts: *idle-booth* when activity stalls, *hype* during the rush, and __system overwatch__ for hardware trouble like charging or connectivity failures.
Rest easy while gear is in the field. You take action when it matters instead of waiting for the bad news.
